description: Keyboard-first photo culling tool for photographers handling large RAW+JPEG shoots (5000+ photos). Built with Tauri 2.x (Rust backend) + Svelte 5 (frontend) + SQLite.

goals.description: Ship MVP with solid architecture — fast iteration but never sacrifice correctness or extensibility
Learn Rust/Svelte/Tauri idioms through real production use
Build correct multi-format architecture (ok if specific format has bugs, architecture must support it)
Full feature set over time without cutting architectural corners

team.name: Ilya Lifshits
team.role: Solo developer — architecture, backend, frontend, testing, docs

techStack.database: SQLite (rusqlite 0.31, bundled, WAL mode, per-project isolation)

testing.methodology: Behavioral TDD with immutable RED tests

architecture.pattern: Tauri 2.x desktop monolith: Rust backend + Svelte frontend
architecture.dataFlow: Frontend keyboard/UI -> invoke() IPC -> Rust handler -> business logic -> SQLite -> status updates

conventions.branchStrategy: sprint-N branches, merge to main after breakpoint review

codeStyle.rust: cargo fmt + clippy before every commit

conventions.errorHandling: thiserror for domain, anyhow for propagation, Result<T, String> for Tauri commands
testRules: Tests must compile (stubs with todo!() for missing API)
RED tests are immutable during GREEN phase
Expected metadata must match actual file content
Rule 9: invoke() default THROWS on unmocked commands

defaultProcess.description: Two-phase behavioral TDD: black-box tests first, then minimum implementation

processPreferences.breakpointPolicy: Major decisions only (architecture, merge to main, destructive ops)

active.description: Bug fix cycles after feature delivery
